manifest.description.message: Search GitHub repos, users, and issues from the launcher. Type "gh <query>" for repos, "gh @<user>" for users, "gh #<query>" for issues.

result.home.label.description: Result label when only the trigger is typed

result.home.description.description: Hint to search after the trigger

result.me.label.description: Result label for opening the user's GitHub profile

result.me.description.message: (uses the token if configured, otherwise github.com home)
result.me.description.description: Hint that token-aware lookup is used

result.searching.label.description: Loading label while async search runs

settings.description.message: Search GitHub from the launcher. Works unauthenticated up to 60 req/hr (per IP). Optional: paste a fine-grained personal access token for 5,000 req/hr and access to private repos. The token is stored locally in Kage's sandboxed extension data.

settings.access.intro_html.message: Generate a fine-grained personal access token at github.com/settings/personal-access-tokens/new. Read-only on the repos / issues / users you want to search is enough. The token never leaves your machine except in the Authorization header to api.github.com.

settings.access.token_saved_html.message: Token saved. Paste a new one to replace, or click Forget token to remove.
settings.access.token_saved_html.description: Shown when a token is already saved

settings.clear_token.confirm.message: Forget the saved GitHub token? Future searches fall back to the 60 req/hr public limit.
settings.clear_token.confirm.description: Confirmation prompt before forgetting the token

action.save_token.empty.description: Status when save action runs without input

action.save_token.invalid.message: ⚠️ Doesn't look like a GitHub token.
action.save_token.invalid.description: Status when token format is unexpected
